title: Why Culture Shouldn't Have a Price Tag
excerpt: Art, music, and creativity have always been gated by wealth. I explore why that's a design problem — and how we can solve it.
content:
Art, music, and creativity have always been gated by wealth. I explore why that's a design problem — and how we can solve it.

When I said 'culture shouldn't only exist for those who can afford it,' I meant it as both a mission statement and a critique of how we've structured access to creative work.

The internet promised to change this. And in some ways, it has. But paywalls, subscriptions, and algorithmic gatekeeping have rebuilt the same walls in digital form.

The answer isn't just free access — it's thoughtful design that respects people's time, attention, and dignity while making culture genuinely accessible.

title: Building Relay: Why Privacy is a Design Choice
excerpt: End-to-end encryption isn't just a feature. It's a philosophy about who owns a conversation.
content:
End-to-end encryption isn't just a feature. It's a philosophy about who owns a conversation.

When we built Relay, we didn't start with 'what features do users want?' We started with 'what does a conversation between two people deserve?'

The answer: complete privacy. No ads built on your data. No metadata sold to third parties. Just messages, encrypted, between the people who wrote them.

This is what privacy-first design looks like in practice.
